channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: TheShy Returns to Competitive LoL and Invictus Gaming
item.content:encoded:
Legendary toplaner Kang ‘TheShy’ Seung-lok has returned to Invictus Gaming (IG) and competitive play ahead of LPL Split 3.

The former League of Legends World Champion has sat out most of the 2026 season, having announced he was taking a break back in December.

TheShy has taken back his place as IG’s starting LPL toplaner, replacing Chen ‘Breathe’ Chen who has now become a free agent.

Substitute toplaner Lim ‘Soboro’ Seong-min and Zou ‘Nia’ Guang-Lu, who shared the mid lane role last split, have also both left the organisation.

TheShy Returns to Rescue IG

IG will be hoping the return of TheShy will help change the team’s fortunes after struggling all year.

They finished 11-12 in the LPL Playoffs last split, being swept 3-0 by ThunderTalk Gaming in the Play-In.

Song ‘Rookie’ Eui-jin, who won LoL Worlds 2018 with TheShy, is still on the IG roster despite sitting out Split 2 and will likely return to the line-up too.

This means the IG roster for LPL Split 3 will be:

  • Top – TheShy
  • Jungle – Wei
  • Mid – Rookie and Renard
  • Bot – JiaQi
  • Support – Meiko

Allowing veteran players to take a rest could be a smart move, as it worked for Top Esports (TES).

TES brought back Gao ‘Tian’ Tian-Liang and Yu ‘JackeyLove’ Wen-Bo for Spring, and the former World Champions helped the team qualify for MSI.

IG will be hoping to do something similar on the road to LoL Worlds 2026.

Last year, IG and TheShy qualified for Worlds but didn’t get to the Main Event after losing to eventual winners T1 in the Play-In.

If BLG continue their fine form, the LPL will have a fourth seed at Worlds and IG will have a huge opportunity to go further than last year.

Especially as the chaotic LPL Playoffs with huge upsets by Team WE proved the league is wide open.

Can TheShy help IG turn their fortunes around? Share your thoughts with us on the Insider Gaming Discord.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Does UK Social Media Ban Affect Gaming? Are Twitch & Kick Banned?
item.content:encoded:
The latest in a series of massive clampdowns by the UK government will see many social media services banned for a certain age group, and there is widespread concern for those affected that gaming services used regularly will be included.

On June 15, at around 8 AM, UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced a ban on certain social media platforms. If you’re wondering which are affected and if it includes gaming platforms or services, read on for full details.

At the time of writing, the UK has not publicly announced that Twitch and Kick are being banned; however, there is an ‘exhaustive list of which platforms’ yet to be announced, and they will likely be on it.

The BBC is quoted as saying:

‘An exhaustive list of which platforms the ban will apply to has not been released. However the government said it would apply to those “whose purpose is to enable social interaction and which allow users to post material”.

The measures will also require platforms to prevent children from being able to livestream, including on gaming platforms.’

This all but confirms that Twitch and Kick livestreaming for Under-16s will be stopped, and they will likely be banned from interacting with the platforms altogether, but this is unofficial for now.

There doesn’t seem to be any further legislation concerning Under-16s and playing on PlayStation, Xbox, PC, or any other gaming platforms.

Never say never with new gaming laws and bans. For now, though, it seems that it’s just social media being targeted. But we are already seeing a ramp-up in age verification concerning multiple gaming services.

  • More Insider Gaming: “Buy Now, Pay Later” Option Appears on XBOX Backend

Sony has stated multiple times that PlayStation Age verification is happening, and we’ve already seen Roblox age verification.

The law on the UK Social Media Ban first needs to be presented and green-lit in parliament, which Starmer is hoping for as soon as late-2026, with a full ‘Spring 2027’ implementation planned.

Once again, I differ to the BBC:

‘The prime minister said the government plans to pass regulations before Christmas. This would allow the ban to be introduced by spring 2027.’

So, there is a chance this might not go through, but the UK Government site claims that this notion is ‘backed by 9 in 10 parents,’ and the chances of it happening are great. 

Some of the biggest social media platforms in the world, including Facebook, X, and TikTok, will be part of the nationwide ban.

Here are the first social media platforms officially confirmed in the ban:

  • Snapchat
  • TikTok
  • YouTube
  • Instagram
  • Facebook
  • X

Again, I stress that there is an unconfirmed list of many other platforms that will be included if the ban is passed. So far, there has been no word on BlueSky, nor WhatsApp.

An official announcement on the full list of platforms to be banned for Under-16s will be revealed later down the line.

UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has cited multiple reasons for the social media ban: Bullying, its addictive nature, and the betterment of child growth and development.

The UK Government website also said this:

‘Children will be given back their childhoods thanks to government action to ban social media platforms from offering services to under-16s, with less time for scrolling and more time for play. 

The plans will set a new normal for future generations, kickstarting a cultural shift and driving forward the government’s fight to give every child the best start in life. 

The government plans to use the same model for a social media ban as Australia. This would capture user-to-user platforms, whose purpose is to enable social interaction and which allow users to post material, alongside algorithms. The ban will therefore include platforms like Snapchat, TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, Facebook and X. We do not intend for messaging services like WhatsApp and Signal to be included in the social media ban.

In a move to protect children online and address the scale of the challenge, the government will also go further than a blanket ban on social media with world-leading blocks on harmful functions such as livestreaming and stranger communication with children for under-16s. These restrictions – which together with the ban go further than any other country – will apply to a wider range of online services, including on gaming sites.’

In short, this is a protective measure to overcome the everyday consumption of social media and the harm it’s doing to newer generations.

  • More Insider Gaming: EA To Bring More In-Game Advertisements With New Branch

Australia introduced its own ban in 2025, and we’ve already seen America briefly take TikTok offline. We could start to see this more and more around the world.

Have your say: Is this a good thing? Do we need to see more regulations in place to give the next generation a brighter future? Let us know through the Insider Gaming Discord.

Check out the former head of Sony Interactive believing gaming’s future is Indie.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

MOBILE APP

item.title: Xbox Game Studios Boss Announces Exit After 20 Months
item.content:encoded:
XBOX Game Studios boss Craig Duncan has announced that he will be stepping down this week after being in the role for 20 months.

According to a report from The Game Business, in addition to the departure of the boss, Chief of Staff Louise O’Connor will also be leaving Xbox Game Studios. Following Duncan’s exit, Xbox Chief Strategy Officer Matt Booty will oversee the department while the company searches for a replacement for the position.

Duncan was responsible for overseeing several development studios, including Halo Studios, The Coalition, Flight Sim, Turn 10, Playground Games, Rare, Obsidian, Ninja Theory, Compulsion Games, The Initiative, Double Fine, InXile, Undead Labs, World’s Edge, and XGS Publishing. He has been with the company since 2011, spending the majority of his time leading Rare.

Louise O’Connor, on the other hand, has been a part of Rare since 1999 and stayed with the company until Everwild was canceled in 2025. O’Connor became the Chief of Staff at XBOX Game Studios in September 2025. Here’s what Craig Duncan stated about his work at XBOX Game Studios and about O’Connor:

“When I stepped into the role of leading XGS 20 months ago, my purpose was to serve our studios, our teams, and the people making our games. Together, we set out to deliver high-quality games, strengthen the cultural fabric across our studios, and help shape the future of the business. I’m proud to say we delivered many flawless launches that drove business success for the company.”

“Louise has been a thoughtful, creative, and trusted partner who has consistently championed the craft and supported our studios with clarity and care. I’m grateful for everything she’s brought to XGS, and I’m sure she’ll be successful in whatever comes next for her.”

In other news, a new ‘buy now, pay later’ option has been spotted on the XBOX backend, hinting that such a feature could arrive soon. Additionally, a new report states that Microsoft loses hundreds of dollars per XBOX Series X/S console sold. Here are more details. What are your thoughts on XBOX Game Studios boss leaving his role after 20 months? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about a claim stating God of War Laufey could come as early as 2027. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: God of War Laufey ‘Could’ Release as Soon as Early 2027, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
A new GOW game starring Faye from the reboot games wasn’t the biggest shock in the world when it was announced during the Sony State of Play Showcase for June 2026. But it would be a surprise if it were released in the next few months, which is apparently a possibility.

The rumored God of War spin-off game was revealed during the Sony State of Play Showcase in June 2026.

As expected, Faye is the main character, temporarily taking over our strong, bearded awarrior—as Santa Monica has said God of War isn’t done with Kratos—and all eyes are on the release date.

God of War Laufey Targeting First Half of 2027?

Now, I must reinforce that this is a claim, and one that ‘could change,’ which is expected as you never know anymore. Release dates are being shifted left, right, and center, even for the biggest games on the market.

However, reliable industry insider NateTheHate was asked on June 13 about God of War Laufey’s release date:

‘Last I heard the target was first half 2027. Could change but that was a hope at one point.’

It’s a bit of a double-edged sword, then, but it does support Jason Schrier’s claim that God of War Laufey will ‘Definitely’ release before 2028.

Rumors suggest Santa Monica has been developing Laufey for some time, so it’s more than reasonable to assume it’s far along in the development cycle.

Whether it releases in early 2027 is one thing, but it seems extremely likely that we’ll get GOW Laufey in 2027. This should keep us all ticking over until the massive God of War original trilogy remake that is also in the works.

If you missed it, check out the first gameplay footage of God of War Laufey.

Head to the Insider Gaming Discord server to discuss GOW Laufey, the upcoming remakes, or anything else gaming-related.

The IG stories don’t end here: First details on The Witcher multiplayer game have reportedly been revealed, a Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 Nintendo Switch 2 release window has been outed, and The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time Remake description has been secretly changed by Nintendo.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Blood of Dawnwalker Will Be ‘Similar to Classic Fallout Games’
item.content:encoded:
At first blush, that’s not a headline that makes much sense. After all, The Blood of Dawnwalker is an ambitious and brutal third-person vampire simulator with serious Witcher vibes, slated to be released this September. How could it possibly be similar to the classic run of Fallout games, a spate of isometric, turn-based post-apocalyptic RPG titles?

In a recent interview, the CEO of Rebel Wolves, the studio behind The Blood of Dawnwalker, one of this year’s most anticipated games, explained the shaping of this title, including post-launch plans.

The Blood of Dawnwalker Starts a Saga

In a recent interview with PAP Biznes, the CEO at Rebel Wolves, Konrad Tomaszkiewicz, sat down to talk about all things The Blood of Dawnwalker. This AAA third-person action title is set to drop on September 3rd, bringing about a game that’s steeped in mystery, innovation, and gruesome vampire-backed combat sequences.

If you’ve seen The Blood of Dawnwalker and think it looks like a Witcher game, you’re not far from the truth. Tomaszkiewicz worked at CDPR, most notably as the director on The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt, released in 2015, and several of the studio’s employees came from that side of the industry.

In the interview, the Rebel Wolves CEO tried to describe the narrative concept in The Blood of Dawnwalker, relying on a series that comes out of left field for this comparison:

‘The Blood of Dawnwalker’ will be primarily a narrative sandbox, similar to the classic ‘Fallout’ games. Our goal is for the player to be able to decide which path they want to take to reach the end of the game.

(Thanks to PushSquare for the spot)

It’s nothing like Fallout in terms of the overarching gameplay elements, visual palette, or setting, but it’s like Fallout in that it has a branching narrative that can be driven by the player at all times.

The hope from Rebel Wolves is that The Blood of Dawnwalker, which has secured a spot on more than 1.5 million wishlists, does well enough at launch that it secures the studio’s financial future, allowing them to expand on the saga that’s waiting to be explored.

There could even be a spate of free DLCs released for The Blood of Dawnwalker after the game hits the market in September, all being well.

Everything is shaping up quite nicely for The Blood of Dawnwalker, and it stands firm as one of this year’s most promising games.

Are you looking forward to The Blood of Dawnwalker? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that ARC Raiders is getting a PvE map condition

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill
